Big water come fast and take the dry ground where the berry grow.
Mountain shake hard and make the stone roof fall on my sleeping furs.
Sky fire hit the tall trees and make the forest smoke hide the sun.
Great wind blow away the skin tent and make the small ones cold.
Earth open wide and swallow the path to the river where the fish swim.
Big fire in sky make night look like day and scare the deer.
Sky-spirit open mouth and spit blue light over the mountain and the trees.
I hold sharp stone tight because the stars dance in way that feel wrong.
Sun-stone fall from high place and make loud boom that shake my cave.
Moon turn red like blood of mammoth and I fear the Great Dark coming.
This cave my cave, you go back to river where big fish jump.
You take stone from my hill, I take club and hit your head.
My fire burn here since winter, you find own hole in the mountain.
You cross line of red clay, you fight me and my strong brothers.
Give back the skin of deer or I push you off the high cliff.
Bright hole in rock make eyes hurt and smell like storm coming.
Me throw stone in glowy water but stone no make sound.
Big light eat the air and make skin feel like cold wind.
Spirit of mountain hide in blue fire and call me to come.
Me hold spear tight because strange light might be hungry tooth beast.
Big leaves hide the path and I must push them with my strong arms.
I hold my sharp stone tight in case the long-tooth cat leaps from shadow.
The ground is soft and wet, making it hard to track the great deer.
I look for the high rock that tells me where the river water flows.
Thorns scratch my skin and pull at my furs while I walk through brush.
Sun go down and come up, but no one make sound in dark.
I sit by cold ash and wait for brother to call my name.
Only drip of water on stone tell me that time still move forward.
Heart beat loud in chest because wind stop and forest grow too quiet.
I hold sharp flint and watch cave mouth for any sign of noise.
Big cat come for me, so I grip heavy stone and scream loud.
You want my fire, but I fight with sharp flint to keep it.
I see saber-tooth in brush and I lift club to protect my kin.
Many strangers come to my cave and I throw rocks to drive them away.
Sky-fire bring smoke and heat, I run fast to find deep stone hole.
This stone is sharp and cuts meat better than any other rock I found.
I keep this heavy fur wrap close to keep warm when cold wind blows.
My spear has the strongest point for hitting the big beast in the woods.
This fire-stone makes sparks fast so I can keep the cave light and warm.
I found this smooth shell by the water and it is most beautiful thing.
Fire-wood gone and cold wind come, must save last dry branch for night.
Water hole get small and dry, we must drink slow or die soon.
Meat from big beast rot fast, must smoke it all or starve when snow.
Sharp stones few in this valley, must use old flake for skinning small rabbit.
Only one torch left for dark place, must walk fast before light go away.
